title: PatchTalk Plugin Decisions
description: Choose the first-version behavior for a JetBrains plugin that adds local review-style comments to the IDE and exposes them to MCP-compatible agents.

decisions.title: Comment Anchoring
decisions.question: How should comments attach to code in the first version?

choices.label: Line + File Comments
choices.description: Support comments on a specific line and comments attached to a file without a line. Smallest reliable scope that still works outside diffs.

choices.label: Line Range + Line + File
choices.description: Support comments on a start/end line range in addition to single-line and file comments. Better for larger review notes, but adds more editor and diff complexity.

choices.label: Code Symbol Anchors
choices.description: Anchor comments to functions, classes, or symbols instead of raw lines. More robust after edits, but requires PSI-aware behavior from the start.

decisions.title: Where Comments Appear
decisions.question: Where should the plugin show and create comments in the IDE?

choices.label: Editor + Diff Viewer
choices.description: Show comments in normal file editors and in JetBrains diff viewers. This matches your review-focused workflow without restricting it to diffs.

choices.label: Editor Only
choices.description: Limit the first version to normal file editors. Faster to build, but diff review would feel incomplete.

choices.label: Editor + Diff + Comment Tool Window
choices.description: Add a dedicated comment inbox/tool window from the first release. Best visibility, but materially larger scope.

decisions.title: Where Comment Data Lives
decisions.question: How should the plugin persist comments?

choices.label: Project-local in .idea
choices.description: Store comments per project in JetBrains project state under .idea. Comments stay with the project and are available to the plugin and MCP when the project is open.

choices.label: Project Sidecar JSON
choices.description: Store comments in a visible JSON file in the project. Easier to inspect manually, but noisier and easier to break.

choices.label: User-local IDE Storage
choices.description: Store comments only in the local IDE profile. Simplest privacy model, but comments do not travel with the project.

decisions.title: Agent Resolution Flow
decisions.question: How much authority should the agent have over comment state in the first version?

choices.label: Reply + Resolve
choices.description: The agent can read comments, add replies, and mark comments resolved. This is the closest to your requested workflow.

choices.label: Reply Only
choices.description: The agent can answer comments but cannot resolve them. Lower risk if you want final control over comment state.

choices.label: Suggest Actions Only
choices.description: The agent can read comments and suggest fixes, but comment state changes stay manual. Lowest automation, highest friction.
